body:
## Issue #511 — case study, requirements, and implementation plan

Closes part of #511 (the "compile data + deep analysis + solution plans"
deliverable, R19/R20). The remaining requirements are decomposed into a sequenced
epic of follow-up issues so each ships and is verified on its own.

### What the issue is
A user in the desktop app asked to list their home directory, then to run
`` `ls ~` `` in the terminal. Both failed — the terminal request dead-ends in the
worker's **`unknown`** fallback. The maintainer's ask is much larger than a bug fix:
surface an **agent / full-auto** mode from the start, with **per-command permission
prompts**, executing real actions via **[link-assistant/agent]** **through**
**[link-assistant/agent-commander]**, isolated in a **Formal-AI Docker container**,
with full integration + e2e tests.

### Key finding
**Most primitives already exist** in the repo — a permission-gated tool router with a
`shell` tool (`desktop/lib/tool-router.cjs`), a Docker sandbox (`konard/box-dind`),
an OpenAI-compatible local server (`FORMAL_AI_DESKTOP_SERVER`), and the
`src/agentic_coding/` loop from #468. Issue #511 is largely an **integration + UX +
onboarding** problem, not a green-field build. The terminal-command request simply has
no handler, and agent mode is off-by-default with no onboarding and no real-CLI wiring.

### What this PR delivers (`docs/case-studies/issue-511/`)
- **`README.md`** — root-cause trace + current-state inventory (reuse-don't-rebuild).
- **`requirements.md`** — full inventory **R1–R20** with source quotes, priority,
  status, acceptance criteria, and a traceability matrix.
- **`solution-plans.md`** — a plan per requirement built on existing components, with
  a build-vs-reuse summary.
- **`proposed-issues.md`** — sequenced epic **E0–E8** (E1 is the smallest hermetic
  slice that visibly fixes the screenshot: a terminal-command intent + a
  chat/agent/full-auto radio group).
- **`raw-data/`** — issue JSON, screenshot, verbatim reasoning trace, the three
  external repo READMEs (refreshed to `agent` v0.24.0 / `agent-commander` v0.6.2), a
  new `external-agent-permissions.md` snapshot of the Agent-CLI permission protocol,
  and an online-research note.

### Upstream re-verification (latest versions, per PR feedback)
Re-checked both upstream repos at their latest versions: **`@link-assistant/agent`
v0.24.0** now ships a native, enforceable permission system (`--permission-mode
auto|plan|readonly|ask`, OpenCode-compatible `--permission` JSON, and a per-command
JSON approval protocol — JS + Rust), resolving the gap I had filed at `agent#271` via
`agent#272`. **`agent-commander` v0.6.2** has not yet exposed it, so two follow-ups
were filed upstream (R16): `agent-commander#39` (map the `agent` tool's read-only onto
its native `--permission-mode`) and `agent-commander#40` (uniform per-command approval
relay). Both are tracked under E8 (#520) and noted as prerequisites in E4 (#516).

### Why a plan, not the whole feature, in one PR
The feature spans three repositories, a new Docker image, and live coding-CLI
integration that — by the issue's own rule — must run **inside an isolated container
with separate subscriptions**, never against the developer's local `claude`/`codex`.
Per the repo's convention for large vision issues (cf. #244 → E1–E34), this PR lands
the plan + first-slice recommendation; each milestone then ships independently.

### Tests
Docs-only change; no code paths modified. A changelog fragment is included
(`bump: patch`).



Fixes #511

commits.messageBody:
Adding .gitkeep for PR creation (default mode).
This file will be removed when the task is complete.

Issue: https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/511

…s, and epic

Compiles the issue data (screenshot, verbatim reasoning trace, the three
link-assistant repo READMEs, online research) into docs/case-studies/issue-511/,
and adds:

- README.md: root-cause trace (terminal-command prompts dead-end in the worker's
  'unknown' fallback) and a current-state inventory showing most primitives
  already exist (permission-gated tool router, Docker sandbox, OpenAI-compatible
  local server, src/agentic_coding/ loop).
- requirements.md: full inventory R1-R20 with source quotes, priority, status,
  acceptance criteria, and a traceability matrix.
- solution-plans.md: per-requirement plans that reuse existing components, with a
  build-vs-reuse summary.
- proposed-issues.md: sequenced epic E0-E8 to ship agent/full-auto mode via
  link-assistant/agent through link-assistant/agent-commander, isolated in a
  Formal-AI Docker container.

Removes the placeholder .gitkeep. Delivers R19/R20 (data compilation + analysis).

Refs #511
commits.messageHeadline: docs(case-study): add issue-511 analysis, requirements, solution plan…

commits.messageBody:
… agent#271

Address review feedback: the proposed epic milestones were only a written
plan; create them as actual GitHub issues via gh and link the case-study
docs to the live issue numbers.

- Create E1–E8 as issues #513–#520 in link-assistant/formal-ai, labeled
  enhancement and linked as sub-issues of #511.
- File the residual upstream gap (the @link-assistant/agent tool has no
  native permission system, so agent-commander --tool agent --read-only is
  rejected) as link-assistant/agent#271. The per-tool read-only enforcement
  for claude/codex/opencode/qwen/gemini was already shipped in
  agent-commander#20 (closed).
- Update README.md, requirements.md (R16), proposed-issues.md, and the
  changelog fragment to reference the created issue numbers.
commits.messageHeadline: docs(case-study): create issue-511 epic issues (#513–#520) + upstream…

commits.messageBody:
…ommander v0.6.2

Re-checked the latest upstream versions per PR #512 feedback:
- agent#271 (no native permission system) is RESOLVED by agent#272 (v0.24.0):
  native --permission-mode auto|plan|readonly|ask, OpenCode-compatible
  --permission JSON policy, and a per-command JSON approval protocol (JS + Rust).
- Filed two residual agent-commander gaps so the full #511 plan is implementable:
  - agent-commander#39: map --read-only/--plan-only for the agent tool onto its
    new native --permission-mode.
  - agent-commander#40: uniform per-command approval relay across tools.

Refreshed all case-study docs (README, requirements R16, solution-plans R9/R12/R16,
proposed-issues E4/E8 + upstream table, online-research §1/§2, version watermark) and
added a new external-agent-permissions.md snapshot of the agent permission protocol.
Updated the changelog fragment to record the re-verification.
commits.messageHeadline: docs(case-study): re-verify issue-511 against agent v0.24.0 + agent-c…
docs(case-study): re-verify issue-511 vs agent-commander js_0.8.0/rus…

commits.messageBody:
Adding .gitkeep for PR creation (default mode).
This file will be removed when the task is complete.

Issue: https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/513

commits.messageHeadline: feat(solver): recognize terminal-command intent in Rust engine (#513)
feat(worker): recognize terminal-command intent in JS engine (#513)

commits.messageBody:
…e (#513)

Mirror the multilingual detection and localized response bodies across both
engines. Adds Hindi/Chinese terminal phrases and run verbs (Chinese verbs are
matched as substrings since the script has no word boundaries) plus hi/zh
agent_suggestion bodies. Integration tests and the worker vm-sandbox experiment
now exercise all four supported languages.
commits.messageHeadline: feat(solver,worker): extend terminal-command intent to Hindi & Chines…

commits.messageBody:
Replace the binary agent toggle with a Chat/Agent/Full-Auto radio group in the
toolbar and drawer. A new `mode` preference is persisted and the legacy
`agentMode` boolean is derived from it (mode !== "chat") for back-compat; old
stores carrying only `agentMode "on"` upgrade to the Agent radio. The topbar
gains a localized mode-status label.

Adds en/ru/zh/hi catalog keys (buttons.fullAuto, titles.fullAuto,
titles.modeGroup, status.mode) with matching i18n-guard coverage, segmented
mode-radio styling, a dedicated issue-513 e2e (terminal intent in ru/en + the
one-click mode switch), and migrates existing specs off the old agent-toggle
selector. Includes the changelog fragment.

Move the four-language terminal-command bodies out of both engines and
into data/seed/multilingual-responses.lino under the agent_suggestion
(Chat mode) and agent_suggestion_active (Agent mode on) intents, with a
{command} placeholder. src/solver_terminal.rs now reads the template via
seed::response_for and the JS worker via answerFor, filling in the
detected command. No per-language wording remains hardcoded in code
(addresses #513 review feedback).
commits.messageHeadline: refactor(#513): source terminal response prose from seed data

commits.messageBody:
…#513)

Phase B of the de-hardcoding work. The terminal/shell phrases, run verbs,
Chinese run verbs, and leading shell tokens that classify a prompt as a
terminal command no longer live as inline const arrays in the Rust solver
or the JS worker. They now live in the canonical data/seed/terminal-commands.lino.

- src/seed/terminal_commands.rs parses the seed into TerminalCommandVocabulary
  (seed::terminal_command_vocabulary), registered via include_str! in embedded.rs.
- src/solver_terminal.rs reads the vocabulary instead of hardcoded arrays.
- src/web/formal_ai_worker.js embeds a byte-identical inline mirror parsed at
  runtime, kept in lockstep by experiments/issue-513-sync-worker-terminal.mjs
  (operation-vocabulary convention, #386), with a --check mode for CI.
commits.messageHeadline: feat(seed): move terminal-command trigger vocabulary into seed data (…

commits.messageBody:
The Phase A/B terminal additions introduced value tokens that resolved to
no defined meaning, reddening the total_closure gate. Remove the comment
header from data/seed/terminal-commands.lino (which the audit tokenizes)
and regenerate data/seed/closure-generated-*.lino via scripts/close-total.py
so every shell token, command-line, agent_suggestion* intent and response
template is grounded as a first-class meaning (audit unresolved_distinct=0,
idempotent). Re-sync the JS worker terminal mirror to the comment-free seed.

Add reasonable per-test, global, expect and navigation/action timeouts to
both Playwright e2e configs so a hung worker/server/deployment aborts CI
promptly (maintainer feedback).
commits.messageHeadline: fix(seed): ground terminal vocab in total-closure + add e2e timeouts

commits.messageBody:
Make the maintainer's directive a strict, CI-enforced requirement:

- Wire the terminal vocabulary worker-mirror parity check into CI
  (release.yml) so the JS worker can never silently re-hardcode trigger
  phrases that have moved into data/seed/terminal-commands.lino.
- Strengthen CONTRIBUTING convention #2 from 'no per-language word lists'
  to the full 'no hardcoded natural language' rule, covering both triggers
  and responses, the meanings <-> naturalization principle, and the CI
  gates that enforce it (total reference-closure + worker-mirror parity).
- Add docs/design/no-hardcoded-natural-language.md: the rule, the
  meanings/naturalization model, the enforcement gates, and a worked
  example (the #513 terminal-command intent).

The total reference-closure gate (cargo test --tests) already enforces
that every seed value token grounds to a meaning; this documents and
extends that enforcement so the mistake is not repeated.
E1: Terminal-command intent + three-way Mode radio (visible fix for #511)

title: Issue #511: case study, requirements, and agent-mode implementation plan
